macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin surges above $79,000 amid short squeeze and easing market fear
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed above $79,000, with reports citing a dramatic liquidation imbalance that hit short positions and helped extend the rally. The move came as broader bearish sentiment in crypto appeared to ease.

items.title: U.S. lawmaker unveils bill to codify a strategic Bitcoin reserve
items.summary: A new bill was introduced in the U.S. to formalize a strategic Bitcoin reserve, drawing bipartisan attention. The proposal adds to the growing policy debate over government-held digital assets.

items.title: Russia advances crypto legislation with sanctions-circumvention provisions
items.summary: Russian lawmakers advanced a sweeping crypto bill that would license exchanges and allow cross-border crypto use while keeping domestic payment restrictions in place. The proposal also includes language aimed at enabling use around sanctions constraints.

items.title: Blockchains and tokenization gain momentum as Securitize plans SPAC merger
items.summary: Tokenization platform Securitize said it plans to go public through a SPAC merger to scale its onchain asset business. The move underscores continued investor interest in real-world asset tokenization infrastructure.

items.title: OCBC launches $526M tokenized gold fund on Solana
items.summary: OCBC unveiled a physical gold fund onchain as $GOLDX, marking Southeast Asia’s first tokenized physical gold fund on a public blockchain. The launch highlights growing adoption of real-world assets on Solana.

items.title: GSR launches actively managed Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana basket ETF on Nasdaq
items.summary: GSR introduced an actively managed crypto basket ETF that includes Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana. The product reflects continued institutional expansion in regulated digital asset investment vehicles.

items.title: Blockchain.com files for U.S. IPO
items.summary: Blockchain.com has filed for a U.S. initial public offering, adding another major crypto company to the public markets pipeline. The filing signals renewed confidence in capital markets access for digital asset firms.

items.title: New macOS malware campaign targets crypto wallets
items.summary: Security researchers warned about a new Mac malware strain called MacSync Stealer that is designed to drain cryptocurrency wallets. The campaign raises ongoing concerns about endpoint security for retail crypto users.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta begins another major restructuring, cutting nearly 8,000 jobs while expanding AI
items.summary: Meta has reportedly started a new global layoff round affecting nearly 8,000 employees, even as it grows its AI divisions and reassigns thousands of staff into AI-focused teams. The move reflects Zuckerberg’s push for an AI-first strategy and a broader tech industry shift toward automation and leaner management.

items.title: Google highlights Gemini Robotics models for real-world robot control
items.summary: Google showcased new Gemini Robotics capabilities that combine high-level reasoning with real-time robotic control, including video and multi-image support and task planning for physical agents. The company says the models are designed to bridge cloud intelligence and the physical world for more autonomous robotics.

items.title: Google announces Flutter 3.44 and Dart 3.12 with more AI-focused tooling
items.summary: Google unveiled Flutter 3.44 and Dart 3.12, highlighting updates aimed at AI-driven app experiences, improved hot reload, and new server prompt templates in Firebase tools. The release also emphasizes performance and developer productivity across modern app and agent workflows.

items.title: Model-selection best practices emphasize cost, latency, and task fit
items.summary: A new hands-on discussion focused on how to compare AI models by pass rate, cost, latency, and token efficiency rather than raw capability alone. The talk highlighted prompt caching and deduplication techniques that can significantly reduce input token usage in production workflows.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War IV announced with September 17 release date
items.summary: The long-awaited RTS sequel has been unveiled, with a firm release date and post-launch roadmap details. The announcement is one of the biggest strategy-game reveals in the latest wave of gaming news.

items.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate – Deathwatch sequel announced for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC
items.summary: A new entry in the Chaos Gate series was revealed across major platforms, expanding Games Workshop's 40K slate. The announcement adds to a busy day of Warhammer game news.

items.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Deathmaster announced at Warhammer Skulls Showcase
items.summary: A new 2D action platformer set in the Age of Sigmar universe was announced during the Warhammer Skulls showcase. It is part of a larger set of Warhammer-related reveals in the last day.

items.title: Xbox schedules Summer Showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7
items.summary: Microsoft has set its summer showcase plans, including a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day presentation. The announcement signals a major upcoming industry event for Xbox fans and developers.

items.title: The first three Resident Evil titles and Breath of Fire IV are now available on Steam
items.summary: Capcom has brought classic versions of Resident Evil 1, 2, 3, and Breath of Fire IV to Steam. The PC release gives fans another way to revisit several legacy survival-horror and RPG titles.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass June 2025 Wave 1 lineup confirmed
items.summary: Microsoft has revealed the first wave of June 2025 Game Pass additions ahead of its upcoming showcase. The lineup continues the service's steady stream of day-one and catalog updates.

items.title: Helldivers 2 teases the new Force of Law Warbond
items.summary: Arrowhead and Sony have revealed a new Warbond coming to Helldivers 2, giving players fresh unlocks and gear. The announcement adds to the shooter’s ongoing post-launch content cadence.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announce Nioh 3
items.summary: Nioh 3 was revealed during Sony’s State of Play, with the soulslike sequel immediately drawing attention from action-RPG fans. The announcement was one of the showcase’s biggest reveals.

items.title: Arc System Works and Sony reveal a new Marvel fighting game
items.summary: A brand-new tag-fighting game featuring Marvel characters was unveiled, adding a major licensed fighter to the pipeline. The announcement is especially notable for fighting-game and Marvel fans.

items.title: Fortnite offers another free skin with a catch
items.summary: Epic Games has revealed a new free cosmetic opportunity for Fortnite players, though it comes with specific conditions. The promotion is the latest in the game’s frequent crossover and reward updates.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold, its most advanced foldable yet
items.summary: Samsung introduced the Galaxy Z TriFold, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame. The launch marks another major step in the foldable-phone race.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android to desktop computers, aiming to scale the mobile OS beyond phones and tablets. The effort could reshape personal computing and increase pressure on Microsoft and Apple.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm announced its latest wearable chip platforms, focusing on battery life, design, and connectivity improvements. The new chips are designed to remove key barriers holding back broader smartwatch and wearable adoption.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond devices and into the web, broadening access to its assistant and moving closer to agent-style workflows. The move intensifies competition with other major digital assistants and platforms.

items.title: AMD posts strong quarter and continues pressure on Intel
items.summary: AMD reported a $9.2 billion quarter, with disciplined execution helping it capitalize on market gaps and strengthen its position against Intel. The results underscore AMD's growing influence across enterprise computing.

items.title: Meta cuts about 10% of staff as it pivots more aggressively toward AI
items.summary: Meta has reportedly laid off around 10% of its workforce as part of a strategic shift toward artificial intelligence. The move reflects continued restructuring across the tech sector.

items.title: OpenAI adds ChatGPT to PowerPoint
items.summary: Users can now generate slides with ChatGPT inside PowerPoint, bringing generative tools directly into a core productivity app. The integration expands AI-assisted content creation in office software.

items.title: AMD unveils Ryzen AI Halo PC and Ryzen AI Max 400 chips
items.summary: AMD introduced a $3,999 Ryzen AI Halo PC and new Ryzen AI Max 400 chips, directly targeting high-end AI PC and workstation segments. The announcement signals intensifying competition in premium computing hardware.
